questions.question: What is the primary difference between a queue and a stack data structure in terms of the order in which elements are added and removed?
questions.hint: Queue elements are added at the end and removed from the front.

questions.question: Explain the process of enqueueing an element in a queue. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Enqueueing involves adding an element to the end of the queue.

questions.question: Compare and contrast a circular queue with a standard queue. When would you use each data structure?
questions.hint: A circular queue is a standard queue with the added feature of wrap-around, allowing elements to be added and removed from both ends.
